chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No. 2674 of 2025 Cr. Bail Application No. 2675 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES Present: Mr. Justice Omar Sial Mr. Justice Miran Muhammad Shah For hearing of bail application 03.11.2025 Mallag Assa Dashti, advocate for both the applicants. Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Additional Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-. OMAR SIAL, J.- Ali Raza and Rajab Ali have sought post arrest bail in crime number 507 of 2025 registered under sections 9(I) 3-B of The Sindh Control of Narcotics Substance Act, 2024 at Police Station Clifton, Karachi. Their earlier bail applications were dismissed vide separate orders dated 19.09.2025 and 30.09.2025 by the learned Sessions Judge/Special Judge (CNS), Karachi-South. 2. Both the applicants Ali Raza and Rajab Ali were arrested on 14.09.2025 by SIP Fazl-ur-Rehman while they were in possession of 680 grams and 510 grams of charas. 3. We have heard the learned counsel for the applicants and the learned Additional Prosecutor General. Our observations are as follows. 4. The offence with which the applicants are charged, although not bailable, falls within the non-prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C. Keeping in mind the principles enunciated in Tariq Bashir and 5 others vs The State (PLD 1985 SC 34), we do not see any exceptional or extraordinary ground to deny the applicants bail. It is also yet to be seen whether the applicants themselves are users of 2 narcotics and, in essence, victims of the drug trade. The State is yet to establish effective rehabilitation institutions for this purpose. The applicants are admitted to bail subject to their furnishing solvent sureties in the sum of Rs. 100,000 each and P.R. bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

The State is yet to establish effective rehabilitation institutions for this purpose. The applicants are admitted to bail subject to their furnishing solvent sureties in the sum of Rs. 100,000 each and P.R. bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. JUDGE JUDGE Saleem/PS
